features.summary: Nike reported Q1 FY2021 results with revenue flat year-over-year on constant currency basis, representing significant improvement from prior quarter. Digital business grew 83% and now represents over 30% of total business, three years ahead of schedule. The company is accelerating its Consumer Direct Acceleration strategy, focusing on owned digital channels and strategic wholesale partners while exiting undifferentiated wholesale. Gross margins declined 90 basis points due to promotional activity to clear excess inventory, but EBIT grew 13% driven by strong cost management.
features.management_outlook: Management expects revenue to be up high-single to low-double digits for the full year, with stronger growth in the second half as supply constraints ease. They anticipate gross margins to be flat for the year despite near-term promotional pressure, with sequential improvement expected in the second half. The company is investing heavily in digital transformation capabilities while maintaining SG&A discipline, expecting the digital shift to drive long-term revenue and earnings growth through higher margins and improved operational efficiency.
features.risks: Key risks include ongoing COVID-19 uncertainty affecting consumer demand and retail traffic recovery patterns across different markets. The company faces near-term margin pressure from promotional activity needed to normalize inventory levels by Q2 end. Supply chain constraints may limit ability to meet strong consumer demand in the near term. The aggressive shift away from undifferentiated wholesale partners in North America represents execution risk as the company reshapes its marketplace strategy.
features.key_drivers: Digital transformation remains the primary growth driver, with owned digital channels delivering significantly higher margins than wholesale. Strong brand momentum is evident in market share gains, particularly in women's and apparel categories. Innovation pipeline continues with successful launches like Space Hippie and NEXT% footwear platforms. The return of organized sports and increased consumer focus on health and wellness are providing tailwinds, while RFID implementation and data analytics capabilities are enabling better inventory management and personalization.
